assertions.description: U2 (AD8044 quad opamp) units 2, 3, and 4 each implement a non-inverting amplifier with Rf=Rg=1k (gain=2). The topology is: non-inv(+) input receives signal (CO, YO, CVO respectively); feedback resistor (R5, R6, R7 = 1k each) from output to inv(-) input; gain-setting resistor (R9, R11, R13 = 1k each) from inv(-) input to GND. The analyzer correctly detects the feedback resistor but does not recognize the gain-setting resistor-to-GND as a non-inverting amplifier pattern, resulting in the catch-all 'transimpedance_or_buffer' label. Output 75R resistors (R2, R3, R4) are video impedance-matching terminations, not feedback elements. Gain should be reported as 2 (6 dB).

U1 (L7805, lib_id Regulator_Linear:L7805) is an ST Microelectronics 78xx-series positive linear voltage regulator with a typical dropout voltage of ~2V. It is a conventional linear regulator, not a Low Dropout (LDO) device. The analyzer reports topology='LDO', which is misleading. The correct classification is 'linear'. This affects designs where the input/output voltage differential matters for proper operating range assessment.

INCORRECT: R16 (47k) connects between net __unnamed_16 (C15 pin 1) and net __unnamed_17 (C12 pin 1, U4 PD pin). C15 and C12 both return to GND, forming a pi-section RC filter. The analyzer traverses R16 from both ends and reports it as two independent RC LPFs: [0] R16+C12 at 7.2 kHz (input __unnamed_16, output __unnamed_17) and [1] R16+C15 at 33.86 Hz (input __unnamed_17, output __unnamed_16). The input/output nets swap between entries, confirming these are the same physical filter detected in both traversal directions. The true pi-filter has C15=100nF and C12=470pF, meaning it is not symmetrical and the 'reversed' detection is spurious.

All 6 inductors (L1-L6, 22uH each) form LC power supply choke filters between power rails: L1 (+12V to +12VA with C1/C3 100uF bulk caps), L2 (+5V to VCC), L3 (+5V to +5VA), L4 (+5V to +5VD), L5 (+5V to +5VP), L6 (+5V to +5VL). Each inductor feeds into a node with decoupling capacitors, creating classic L-C power filters for EMI isolation between analog and digital supply domains. The analyzer reports lc_filters=0 despite these well-formed LC structures being present in the power distribution network.
